item.title: CK Hutchison takes Maersk to arbitration over Panama port
item.description: Hong Kong conglomerate escalates legal campaign over its ejection from canal terminals

item.title: Shell’s oil traders get earnings boost from Iran war
item.description: FTSE 100 group expects a drop in gas production because of hit to facilities in Qatar

item.title: US and Iran agree 2-week ceasefire that will open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Agreement sparks relief rally in global stock markets as oil price tumbles

item.title: Earnings expectations are too high
item.description: Plus AI and jobs

item.title: FirstFT: Trump and Iran announce 2-week ceasefire
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Turkey’s gold sales and French miner Eramet

item.title: Private equity’s secondaries magic
item.description: Last year investors ploughed a record $166bn into the funds backing ageing assets

item.title: Long Covid to cost OECD economies up to $135bn a year
item.description: New research predicts impact of lower productivity and workers quitting because of long-term illness after Covid-19

item.title: When persistence prevails
item.description: After 593 monthly editions, a legend of role-playing games magazines has finally called it a day

item.title: Eramet’s top shareholder weighs exit as $500mn capital raise looms
item.description: Duval family hires Lazard to advise on 37% stake in troubled mining company

item.title: Perplexity revenue jumps 50% in pivot from search to AI agents
item.description: San Francisco-based start-up surges from push into more complex and potentially more lucrative AI services

item.title: US carmakers accuse EU of blocking supersized pick-up trucks from roads
item.description: Brussels is proposing to tighten safety rules that apply to a small number of specialist imports

item.title: How can we protect my mother’s estate after she passes away?
item.description: Her new partner lives with her and she wants to grant him the right to remain living in the property

item.title: How private equity became hooked on second-hand deals
item.description: Elite private capital firms have piled into the secondaries market as they race to attract assets

item.title: The hangover from Britain’s student housing boom
item.description: Concerns about affordability and community impact are mounting, even as purpose-built accommodation gains traction elsewhere

item.title: Will the Iran war derail the energy transition?
item.description: Countries in Asia and Europe are turning to coal to power their economies but analysts argue solar will be a long-term winner

item.title: London Borough Yards development handed to lender amid struggle to find tenants
item.description: Scheme’s difficulties signal that fallout from pandemic is still being felt in real estate sector

item.title: The dystopian fantasy of uselessness
item.description: Fears that AI will strip our lives of meaning are based on three fallacies

item.title: The south-east Asian country at the sharp end of the energy crisis
item.description: In the Philippines, surging fuel prices have forced a rethink of energy policy and even relations with China

item.title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing offers to buy Universal Music in €55bn deal
item.description: Proposed transaction would combine world’s largest music group with a blank-cheque company

item.title: Musk struggles to crack the Indian market
item.description: More diplomacy by entrepreneur might be needed amid regulatory challenges and domestic competition to his businesses

item.title: Italian government poised to replace Leonardo’s CEO who oversaw order surge
item.description: Move comes as Roberto Cingolani’s three-year term at defence group expires next month

item.title: Anthropic rolls out cyber AI model days after source code leak
item.description: Claude Mythos opened to Amazon, Microsoft and Apple to detect hidden software vulnerabilities

item.title: Bill Ackman strikes a chord with Universal bid
item.description: But financier needs the backing of Vincent Bolloré and his Vivendi media group

item.title: Aldermore Bank put up for sale after owner attacks UK car finance redress scheme
item.description: South Africa’s FirstRand says industry-wide £9.1bn programme is ‘deeply flawed’

item.title: Russian military hackers reroute British internet users’ traffic
item.description: UK National Cyber Security Centre warns that vulnerable routers will let attackers steal passwords and log-in details

item.title: Why an airport water-art display may spark US airline consolidation
item.description: JetBlue’s viability as a standalone entity hinges on a plethora of factors it has little control over

item.title: Commerzbank held talks with UniCredit over potential deal
item.description: German bank hits back after Italian lender accused it of failing to engage

item.title: IMF warns of emerging markets’ exposure to ‘flighty’ hedge funds
item.description: Non-bank lenders rapidly reduce holdings of EM debt during shocks such as the Iran war, analysis suggests

item.title: Hedge funds make record bets against European stocks
item.description: Short positions rise sharply as traders eye economic fallout from Iran war

item.title: BP chair faces re-election battle after board blocks climate resolution
item.description: Glass Lewis recommends voting against Albert Manifold at annual meeting, while L&G also plans to oppose him
